as.english: used in comparisons to mean to the same degree

examples.english: She is as tall as her brother.

as.english: in the role or function of

examples.english: As I was leaving, the phone rang.
As it was late, we went home.
examples.chinese: 因為時間晚了，我們回家了。

by.english: not later than a particular time
used to show the person or thing that does an action

examples.english: The book was written by a doctor.
examples.chinese: 這本書由一位醫生寫成。

one.english: a person, especially people in general

step.english: a movement made by lifting and putting down a foot

examples.english: The first step is to save the file.
examples.chinese: 第一個步驟是儲存檔案。

step.english: to move by lifting and putting down a foot

examples.english: Do not step on the grass.

thick.english: having a large distance between opposite sides
growing close together or difficult to see through

examples.english: The forest was thick and dark.

bite.english: to use the teeth to cut into something

examples.chinese: 那隻狗可能會咬陌生人。

bite.english: an injury made by teeth, or a small amount of food cut by teeth

examples.english: He took a bite of the apple.

get.english: to receive, obtain, or buy something

examples.english: I need to get a new phone.
examples.chinese: 我需要得到一部新手機。

examples.english: What time did you get home?

of.english: used to show belonging or connection

examples.english: This is the cover of the book.

of.english: used to show what something is made from or contains

examples.english: The cup is made of glass.

on.english: touching and supported by a surface

examples.english: The keys are on the table.

west.english: the direction where the sun sets, or the western part of an area

examples.english: The sun sets in the west.
They live on the west coast.

cloud.english: a white or grey mass of very small drops of water in the sky

examples.chinese: 憤怒會使你的判斷模糊。

down.english: towards or in a lower position
from a higher to a lower point along something

examples.english: He felt down after the exam.

dry.english: to remove water from something or become free of water

examples.english: Dry your hands with this towel.
examples.chinese: 用這條毛巾使雙手變乾。

fruit.english: the sweet part of a plant that contains seeds and can be eaten

gate.english: a movable barrier used to close an opening in a fence or wall

examples.chinese: 請把你身後的大門關上。

net.english: material made of strings tied together with spaces between them

examples.english: The fish were caught in a net.

net.english: remaining after costs or taxes have been taken away

north.english: the direction to your left when facing the rising sun, or the northern part of an area

examples.english: The town lies to the north.

package.english: an object wrapped for sending, or a set of related things sold together

examples.chinese: 今天早上有一個包裹送到。

package.english: to put something in a box or wrapping

examples.english: The cookies are packaged in small bags.

tail.english: the part that sticks out at the back of an animal’s body

examples.english: She is a woman with great courage.
examples.chinese: 她是一位具有勇氣的女性。

acid.english: a chemical substance that can react with metals and has a sour taste
containing acid or having the qualities of an acid

at.english: used to show a place, point, or time

examples.english: Meet me at the station at six.

bubble.english: a ball of air or gas in liquid or a thin film

examples.english: Put a dot at the end of the line.

dot.english: to put small marks or objects in many places

double.english: twice as much or as many
to become or make something twice as large

dress.english: a piece of clothing for women or girls that covers the body and part of the legs

examples.chinese: 她穿了一條紅色連身裙。

dress.english: to put clothes on yourself or someone else

flesh.english: the soft part of a body between skin and bone

examples.english: The thorn went into his flesh.

get out.english: to leave or escape from a place

examples.english: We need to get out of the building.
examples.chinese: 我們需要離開這棟大樓。

no.english: used to give a negative answer

node.english: a point where lines or systems meet, especially in a network

examples.english: Each computer is a node on the network.
examples.chinese: 每部電腦都是網絡上的節點。

examples.english: The location is a big plus.

rank.english: a position in an organization or scale

examples.english: He rose to the rank of captain.

rank.english: to have or give a position in an order

examples.english: The school ranks first in the city.
examples.chinese: 這所學校在全市排名第一。

roar.english: to make a very loud deep sound

examples.english: We heard the roar of the engine.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到引擎的轟鳴聲。

shell.english: the hard outer covering of an egg, nut, or animal
an explosive projectile fired from a large gun

sweet.english: a small piece of sweet food

track.english: a path, road, or line used for travel or racing
to follow the movement or progress of someone or something

examples.english: The app can track your steps.
examples.chinese: 這個應用程式可以追蹤你的步數。

examples.english: The cat is under the table.

under.english: less than a particular amount or level

examples.chinese: 五歲以下兒童免費入場。

examples.english: A lamp hung above the table.

above.english: earlier in a piece of writing

all.english: the whole amount or every one of a group

examples.chinese: 所有的學生都必須出席。

all right.english: used to agree or to show acceptance

band.english: a group of musicians who play together
a thin flat strip used for fastening or holding things

examples.english: Tie it with a rubber band.

between.english: in the space separating two people or things

examples.english: The bank is between the school and the shop.
examples.chinese: 銀行在學校和商店之間。

branch.english: a part of a tree growing from the trunk, or a local office of a business

examples.english: The bank opened a new branch.
The road branches after the bridge.

connect.english: to join two or more things together

examples.english: Connect the cable to the computer.

cool.english: slightly cold in a pleasant way
to become less hot or make something less hot

examples.english: Let the soup cool before eating.

degree.english: the amount or level of something

examples.english: The degree of risk is low.

degree.english: a qualification given by a university

examples.english: She has a degree in law.

fire.english: flames and heat, or an uncontrolled burning
to dismiss someone from a job
to shoot a gun or weapon

examples.english: The soldiers fired at the target.
The lake was covered with ice.

into.english: to the inside of a place or thing
used to show a change of state

lead.english: to guide or be in charge of someone or something

leaf.english: a flat green part of a plant

examples.english: A leaf fell from the tree.
The truck carried a heavy load.

load.english: to put things into a vehicle or container

examples.english: They loaded boxes onto the truck.
examples.chinese: 他們把箱子裝載到卡車上。

examples.english: Stand in the middle of the room.

paint.english: a coloured liquid put on a surface
to cover a surface with paint or make a picture with paint

examples.english: The ring is made of silver.
I could not find a single mistake.

single.english: a song released separately from an album

examples.english: The singer released a new single.
examples.chinese: 那位歌手發行了一首新單曲。

examples.english: It was raining, so we stayed home.
examples.chinese: 下雨了，所以我們留在家裏。

tower.english: a tall narrow building or structure

transfer.english: to move from one place, job, or vehicle to another

examples.english: Transfer the files to your laptop.
examples.chinese: 把檔案轉移到你的筆記型電腦。

transfer.english: the act of moving from one place or vehicle to another

advance.english: to move forward or make progress
progress or money paid before it is due

examples.chinese: 醫學進步拯救了許多生命。

against.english: opposing or disagreeing with someone or something

examples.english: Many people voted against the plan.
examples.chinese: 許多人投票反對這個計劃。

examples.english: The course is for children aged five to seven.
examples.chinese: 這課程適合五至七歲的兒童。

examples.english: She cared for her aged parents.

ancient.english: belonging to a very distant past

angle.english: the space between two lines or surfaces that meet
to place something so that it points in a particular direction

examples.english: Angle the lamp toward the desk.

before.english: earlier than a time or event

examples.chinese: 你離開以前打電話給我。

behind.english: at or to the back of someone or something

examples.english: The garden is behind the house.

below.english: later in a piece of writing

bill.english: a document showing how much money is owed

examples.english: Can we have the bill, please?

bond.english: a close connection, or a certificate of debt

examples.english: There is a strong bond between them.
examples.chinese: 他們之間有很強的聯繫。

examples.english: The team bonded during the trip.
examples.chinese: 團隊在旅途中建立關係。

burn.english: to be on fire, or to damage with fire or heat

examples.english: Be careful not to burn your hand.

burn.english: an injury caused by heat or fire

examples.chinese: 在緊急情況下保持平靜。

calm.english: to make someone or something peaceful

can.english: to be able to do something
a metal container for food or drink

cent.english: a unit of money equal to one hundredth of a dollar or euro

circle.english: a round shape with every point the same distance from the centre

examples.english: Draw a circle on the paper.

collect.english: to bring things together or gather them over time

column.english: a vertical section of text or numbers, or a tall support

examples.english: Write the total in the last column.

conduct.english: to organize and carry out an activity, or direct musicians

constant.english: happening all the time or repeatedly

examples.english: The constant noise made sleep difficult.
examples.chinese: 持續的噪音令人難以入睡。

constant.english: a number or quantity that does not change

examples.english: In this formula, k is a constant.
examples.chinese: 在這個公式中，k 是常數。
她吃了一塊朱古力餅乾。

cry.english: to produce tears, or to shout loudly

examples.english: We heard a cry for help.

deliver.english: to take goods or messages somewhere, or give a speech

examples.chinese: 他們每天晚上遞送食物。

divide.english: to separate into parts, or calculate how many times one number contains another

examples.english: Divide the cake into six pieces.

enough.english: as much or as many as needed

feed.english: to give food to a person or animal, or supply something

flight.english: a journey by air, or the act of flying

frame.english: a border or structure that supports something

examples.english: The photo is in a wooden frame.

frame.english: to put a frame around something or express something in a particular way

freeze.english: to become ice or make something very cold and solid

examples.english: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.

grass.english: a common green plant with narrow leaves, or an area covered by it

examples.english: Do not walk on the grass.
Heat the oil in a pan.

heel.english: the back part of the foot or shoe

hole.english: an empty space in a surface or object

examples.english: There is a hole in my sock.

hollow.english: a low or empty area in a surface

image.english: a picture, or the idea people have of someone or something

examples.english: The screen showed a clear image.

knife.english: a tool with a sharp blade used for cutting

examples.english: Cut the bread with a knife.

loose.english: not firmly fixed or not tight

metal.english: a hard shiny material such as iron, gold, or silver

examples.english: The door is made of metal.

needle.english: a small thin pointed tool used for sewing or medical injections

pair.english: two things used together or two people connected in some way

examples.english: I bought a pair of shoes.

path.english: a way or track for walking, or a route to a goal

examples.english: Follow the path through the forest.

pig.english: a farm animal with short legs and a flat nose

examples.english: The pig rolled in the mud.

pipe.english: a tube that carries liquid or gas, or a tube used for smoking

quiet.english: to make someone or something quiet

return.english: to go or come back, or give something back
the act of going back or giving something back

examples.chinese: 她的返回令所有人驚訝。

salt.english: a white substance used to flavour food

sand.english: very small grains of rock found on beaches and deserts

examples.english: The children played in the sand.

size.english: how large or small something is

examples.english: What size shoes do you wear?

slow.english: to become slower or make something slower

examples.english: The car slowed near the school.

solid.english: firm and hard, or not liquid or gas
a substance that is not a liquid or gas

some.english: an amount or number of something, not stated exactly
an amount or number of people or things

source.english: the place, person, or thing something comes from

examples.english: What is the source of this information?
examples.chinese: 這些資料的來源是甚麼？

stick.english: to become fixed to something, or push something into something

examples.english: The label will stick to the box.

surface.english: the outside or top layer of something

examples.english: Clean the surface of the table.

tender.english: gentle and kind, or easy to cut or chew

ticket.english: a printed or electronic document giving permission to travel or enter, or a notice of a fine

tire.english: to become tired or make someone tired
a thick rubber ring around a wheel

examples.english: The car needs a new tire.
examples.chinese: 這輛車需要一個新輪胎。

tool.english: an object used to do a job

examples.english: A hammer is a useful tool.

total.english: to add up to an amount

tube.english: a long hollow object, usually round

examples.english: The cream comes in a tube.
She felt weak after the illness.

yellow.english: having the colour of lemons or ripe bananas
the colour of lemons or ripe bananas

examples.chinese: 黃色是我最喜歡的顏色。
我們談了關於這個計劃的事。

after.english: later than a time or event

examples.chinese: 你到達以後打電話給我。

aid.english: help, especially in a difficult situation

examples.english: The money will aid poor families.
examples.chinese: 這筆錢會援助貧困家庭。

back.english: the part of the body opposite the chest, or the rear part of something
to or in a previous place or condition
to support a person or plan

bark.english: the outer covering of a tree

examples.english: The bark of this tree is rough.

bark.english: to make the short loud sound of a dog

beauty.english: the quality of being beautiful, or a beautiful person or thing

examples.english: Everyone admired the beauty of the lake.

bitter.english: having a sharp unpleasant taste, or full of anger and sadness

block.english: a solid piece of material, or an area surrounded by streets

examples.english: A fallen tree blocked the road.
examples.chinese: 一棵倒下的樹阻擋了道路。

bow.english: to bend your head or body forward as a greeting or sign of respect

examples.english: The actor bowed to the audience.

bow.english: a weapon for shooting arrows, or a knot with two loops

examples.english: She tied the ribbon in a bow.

brick.english: a hard block of baked clay used for building

examples.english: The wall is made of brick.
The bull stood in the field.

cap.english: a soft flat hat, or a cover for the top of a container

examples.english: Put the cap back on the bottle.

cap.english: to put an upper limit on something

chicken.english: a common farm bird, or its meat

close.english: to shut something or become shut
near in distance, time, or relationship

cloth.english: material made by weaving or knitting threads

examples.english: Wipe the table with a cloth.

coarse.english: rough in texture, or rude and offensive

collar.english: the part of a shirt around the neck, or a band around an animal’s neck

examples.english: The dog wore a red collar.

collection.english: a group of things gathered together, or the act of gathering them

examples.english: She has a collection of stamps.

come out.english: to appear, become available, or leave a place

examples.english: His new book will come out next month.

convey.english: to communicate an idea or transport something

cord.english: a long thin piece of string or covered wire

examples.english: Tie the box with a cord.

count.english: to say numbers in order or find the total number
the act of counting or the total number

couple.english: two people or things, or a small number

examples.english: The engine is coupled to the wheels.

cow.english: an adult female animal kept for milk or meat

examples.english: The cow gives milk every day.

crane.english: a tall machine for lifting heavy things, or a large long-legged bird

examples.english: The crane lifted the steel beam.

crew.english: the people who work on a ship, aircraft, or project

examples.chinese: 全體機組人員準備飛機。

current.english: a flow of water, air, or electricity

difference.english: the way in which two people or things are not the same

examples.english: There is a big difference between them.
